conflicts.title: Structural Beam Conflicts with HVAC Supply Duct Run
conflicts.description: The main HVAC supply trunk duct shown on M1 at the second floor runs directly through the path of a W10x22 structural steel beam shown on S2. The beam spans the living area at elevation 9'-6" and the duct requires 14" of vertical clearance at the same location. This conflict was identified across the May and July submittals with no resolution noted. The 5 correction rounds suggest this was flagged but the coordination remains unresolved.
conflicts.recommended_action: Schedule coordination meeting between structural engineer and mechanical contractor. Options: (1) Lower duct to run beneath beam with a bulkhead soffit, reducing ceiling height to 8'-2" locally, or (2) Reroute duct through adjacent hallway. Option 2 preferred to maintain open ceiling in living area.

conflicts.title: Electrical Panel Clearance Violation at Garage Wall
conflicts.description: The main electrical panel (200A) shown on E1 is located on the west garage wall at a position that places it 28 inches from the adjacent water heater shown on P1. NEC 110.26 requires a minimum 36-inch clear workspace in front of the panel. The water heater encroaches into this clearance zone. Additionally, structural bracing shown on S1 further restricts the available workspace.
conflicts.recommended_action: Relocate electrical panel 4 feet north along the garage west wall to achieve required 36-inch clearance. Verify new location does not conflict with garage door framing shown on A1. Coordinate with plumbing contractor to confirm water heater remains accessible.

conflicts.title: Plumbing Vent Stack Routing Through Roof Truss
conflicts.description: The 3-inch plumbing vent stack serving the master bathroom fixtures on P2 is routed vertically through the roof at a location that intersects with a roof truss web member shown on S5 and S7. The truss cannot be field-modified without engineering review. Two versions of S7 exist in the submittal set ([239062] and [239154]), suggesting this area was revised but the plumbing routing was not updated to match.
conflicts.recommended_action: Offset plumbing vent stack 18 inches east to pass between truss web members. Requires re-routing the horizontal vent connection under the floor. Structural engineer must confirm clear zone. Do not field-cut any truss members.

conflicts.title: AC Condensate Drain Conflicts with Foundation Footing
conflicts.description: The AC condensate drain line shown on M2 exits the mechanical closet at grade level and routes through an area where the continuous foundation footing shown on S4 is located. The drain line cannot penetrate the footing without structural approval. The AC calculations document specifies a 3-ton unit requiring a 3/4" condensate line with proper slope, but the current routing lacks adequate fall due to the footing obstruction.
conflicts.recommended_action: Route condensate drain above the footing using a condensate pump, or redirect the drain line 6 feet south to exit through the exterior wall above the footing line. Condensate pump option is simpler but adds a maintenance item. Coordinate with structural.

conflicts.title: Landscape Irrigation Line Crosses Underground Electrical Feed
conflicts.description: The landscape irrigation main line shown on L-1 crosses the underground electrical service lateral shown on E1 at the front yard east side. The civil site plan on CV1 does not show either utility, indicating a coordination gap between landscape, electrical, and civil disciplines. NEC requires minimum 12-inch vertical separation between irrigation and electrical conduits.
conflicts.recommended_action: Add irrigation line and electrical lateral to civil utility plan (CV1). Ensure minimum 12-inch vertical separation at crossing point. If separation cannot be achieved, sleeve the irrigation line with Schedule 40 PVC at the crossing. Landscape contractor to coordinate with electrician before trenching.

conflicts.title: Window Header Size Mismatch Between Architectural and Structural
conflicts.description: Architectural elevations on A3 show (3) 6'-0" wide impact windows on the east second-floor elevation, but the structural plan on S6 specifies headers sized for 4'-0" openings at those locations. The signed and sealed structural set (S6[239067]) does not match the architectural window schedule. In Surfside FL, impact-rated windows require properly sized headers to meet Miami-Dade wind load requirements.
conflicts.recommended_action: Structural engineer to recheck header sizing for 6'-0" openings per Miami-Dade wind load requirements (HVHZ). Likely requires upgrading from (2) 2x10 to (2) 2x12 or LVL beam. Window supplier to confirm frame dimensions match structural opening. This must be resolved before framing begins.

conflicts.title: Bathroom Exhaust Fan Ducting Lacks Exterior Termination Point
conflicts.description: Mechanical plan M3 shows bathroom exhaust fans in both second-floor bathrooms but does not show duct routing to an exterior termination point. Architectural roof plan A4 does not show any roof caps or wall vents for bathroom exhaust. IRC M1501.1 requires all exhaust fans to terminate to the exterior. Without a defined path, the installer will need to improvise routing, risking conflicts with roof trusses.
conflicts.recommended_action: Add exhaust duct routing to M3 showing path from each fan to a roof cap or soffit vent. Coordinate with structural to confirm duct path clears roof trusses (reference S5, S7). Add roof caps to A4 roof plan. Preferred termination: through roof with proper flashing.

conflicts.title: Recessed Light Fixtures Conflict with Ceiling Joist Locations
conflicts.location: Kitchen and Dining Area, First Floor
conflicts.description: The electrical reflected ceiling plan on E3 shows 6 recessed LED downlights in the kitchen and dining area at locations that coincide with ceiling joist positions shown on S2. IC-rated recessed fixtures require a minimum 3" clearance from framing members. At least 3 of the 6 fixtures appear to be directly beneath joists based on the structural framing layout.
conflicts.recommended_action: Shift affected recessed fixtures 6-8 inches to fall between ceiling joists. Electrician to verify final positions against structural framing before rough-in. Minor adjustment — can be resolved during electrical rough-in coordination with framing contractor.

conflicts.title: Stormwater Drainage Grade Conflicts with Driveway Elevation
conflicts.location: Front of Property, Driveway to Street
conflicts.description: The civil site plan CV1 shows finished grade elevations that create a negative slope from the property toward the driveway, potentially directing stormwater toward the garage entry rather than to the street swale. The architectural site plan on A1 shows a different finished floor elevation than CV1, creating a 4-inch discrepancy that affects the drainage design. The survey with trees document shows existing grade conditions that differ from both plans.
conflicts.recommended_action: Civil engineer to reconcile finished floor elevation between CV1 and A1. Verify positive drainage slope from building to street at minimum 2% grade. May require adjusting driveway approach elevation or adding a trench drain at garage entry. Reference survey for existing conditions.
